title: Node.js on Android — Complete Development Guide | Acode
description: Run Node.js on your Android phone with Acode. Install Node.js and npm via the Alpine Linux terminal. Build, run, and deploy Node.js applications directly from your phone. Full development guide.
heroHeadline: Run Node.js on Your Android Phone
heroSubheadline: Acode gives you a full Node.js development environment on Android. Install Node.js and npm in the Alpine Linux terminal, edit code with syntax highlighting, run your apps — all on your phone.
problemHeadline: Node.js Development Is Desktop-Only — Until Now
problemPoints: Node.js is the most popular JavaScript runtime — powering millions of web servers, APIs, and tools. But running Node.js on Android has always been difficult. You need a real terminal, a package manager, and the ability to install native packages.
Regular Android code editors can display JavaScript code, but they can't run it. You can write console.log('hello') but you can never see the output. That's not development — that's typing.
Termux can run Node.js, but without a proper code editor, you're stuck editing JavaScript in Vim on a touchscreen. No syntax-aware editing, no project management, no debugging console.
solutionHeadline: Full Node.js Environment on Android with Acode
solutionPoints: Install Node.js and npm in Acode's terminal with a single command: apk add nodejs npm. That's it. Node.js is now running natively on your Android phone — no cloud, no remote server, no compromises.

Write Node.js code in Acode's editor with full JavaScript syntax highlighting, code completion, and IntelliSense. Create Express servers, build CLI tools, write automation scripts — exactly as you would on a desktop.
Use npm to install any package from the registry. npm install express, npm init, npm run dev — they all work. Run npm scripts, manage dependencies, and use npx for one-off commands.
Debug your Node.js apps with Acode's interactive JavaScript console, terminal-based Node.js debugging, and console.log output visible in real-time. The complete Node.js development loop on Android.

setupSteps.title: Install Acode
setupSteps.description: Get Acode from Google Play or F-Droid.

setupSteps.title: Open the Terminal
setupSteps.description: Tap the terminal icon. Alpine Linux will set up on first launch (~60 seconds).

setupSteps.title: Install Node.js and npm
setupSteps.description: Run: apk update && apk add nodejs npm. This installs the latest Node.js and npm available in Alpine's repository.

setupSteps.title: Verify Installation
setupSteps.description: Run: node --version and npm --version. You should see the installed versions.

setupSteps.title: Create Your First Project
setupSteps.description: Run: mkdir my-project && cd my-project && npm init -y. Then npm install express to add Express.

setupSteps.title: Code and Run
setupSteps.description: Create index.js in Acode's editor with Express server code. Run node index.js in the terminal. Your server is now running on your phone.

features.title: Express & Web Servers
features.description: Build REST APIs, web servers, and full-stack applications with Express, Fastify, or Koa. All npm packages that work on Alpine Linux work on Acode.

features.title: npm Package Management
features.description: Install any npm package that's compatible with Node.js on Alpine. npm install, npm update, npm audit — it all works.

features.title: CLI Tools
features.description: Build and run CLI tools with Node.js. Use commander, inquirer, chalk — all the familiar libraries.

features.title: npm Scripts
features.description: Run build processes, tests, linters, and formatters through npm scripts. Use npm run build, npm test — just like on desktop.

features.title: npx Support
features.description: Run one-off commands with npx: npx create-react-app my-app, npx eslint ., npx prettier --write ..

features.title: JavaScript Console
features.description: Acode's interactive JS console lets you test snippets, debug variables, and experiment with APIs without creating files.

faqs.question: Does Node.js run natively on Android?
faqs.answer: Yes, through Acode's Alpine Linux terminal. Node.js runs in a Linux environment (via proot) on your Android device. It's not an emulator or simulator — it's real Node.js.

faqs.question: Can I install any npm package?
faqs.answer: Most npm packages work. Packages with native C++ addons may need Alpine-compatible binaries. Pure JavaScript packages work universally. Test with npm install — if it compiles, it works.

faqs.question: Can I run a production Node.js server on Android?
faqs.answer: Acode is designed for development, not production hosting. You can run a Node.js server for testing and development, but for production, deploy to a cloud server or VPS.

faqs.question: Is Node.js performance different on Android?
faqs.answer: There is a small overhead from proot, but it's minimal. For development and testing, performance is excellent. Modern Android phones have more than enough power for Node.js development.

faqs.question: Can I use nvm on Acode?
faqs.answer: nvm may work but the Alpine package manager provides Node.js versions directly. Use apk search nodejs to see available versions.
